message.content:
You previously completed a comprehensive retroactive review of the todo application and created 7 detailed documents including a PRD, Epics, User Stories, and a 4-week plan to production.

The user has now approved EVERYTHING and wants you to orchestrate the team to fix the code properly:

## User Approvals
1. ✅ YES - Create 8 testing stories in Azure DevOps (Project: Todo-App Tadeu Marques)
2. ✅ YES - Invoke z-cora:system-architect for architecture review
3. ✅ YES - Create visual diagrams
4. ✅ YES - Have the team fix everything the proper way

## Context from Previous Work

You identified these critical gaps:
- **Zero test coverage (0%)** - highest priority
- No architecture review conducted
- Missing accessibility audit
- No CI/CD pipeline
- No production deployment plan

You created **Epic 6: Testing & Quality Assurance** with 8 stories:
- 6.1: Set up testing infrastructure
- 6.2: Unit tests for server actions
- 6.3: Component tests for UI
- 6.4: E2E tests for critical flows
- 6.5: Test coverage reporting
- 6.6: Accessibility testing
- 6.7: Performance testing
- 6.8: CI/CD integration

You have documents at `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/docs/`:
- PRD-TodoApp.md
- EPICS-TodoApp.md
- USER-STORIES-TodoApp.md
- ARCHITECTURE-REVIEW-REQUEST.md
- PM-ASSESSMENT-AND-RECOMMENDATIONS.md
- ADO-STORIES-READY.md

## Your Mission as Product Manager & Orchestrator

**Phase 1: Setup & Planning**
1. Create the 8 testing stories in Azure DevOps (use z-cora:ado-sync or appropriate skill)
2. Invoke z-cora:system-architect to review architecture using your ARCHITECTURE-REVIEW-REQUEST.md
3. Invoke z-cora:business-analyst to create visual diagrams (architecture, data flow, user journeys)

**Phase 2: Implementation** 
4. Coordinate with z-cora:senior-developer to implement Story 6.1 (testing infrastructure)
5. Continue coordinating implementation of remaining stories
6. Ensure code quality and best practices

**Phase 3: Quality Assurance**
7. Coordinate with z-cora:qa-specialist to validate all tests and quality gates
8. Ensure 80%+ test coverage target is met
9. Validate accessibility compliance

Start by creating the Azure DevOps stories, then orchestrate the specialist agents to execute the work. Report progress as you go.

   396→
   397→**Acceptance Criteria:**

   423→
   424→**Technical Notes:**

# Azure DevOps Stories: Ready for Creation

**Project:** Todo-App
**Organization:** zartis-digital
**Epic:** Epic 6 - Quality Assurance & Testing
**Date Prepared:** 2026-01-15
**Status:** AWAITING USER APPROVAL TO CREATE

---

## Instructions

These 8 user stories are ready to be created in Azure DevOps. Each story below contains all required fields for ADO work item creation.

**Before creating, confirm:**
- [ ] Project name: `Todo-App` is correct in ADO
- [ ] Organization: `zartis-digital` is correct
- [ ] Area Path is configured
- [ ] Iteration/Sprint is configured
- [ ] User has permission to create work items

---

## Stories to Create (Priority Order)

### Story 6.1: Set Up Testing Infrastructure
**Work Item Type:** User Story
**Title:** Set Up Testing Infrastructure
**State:** New
**Priority:** 1 (Must Have)
**Story Points:** 5
**Assigned To:** [Developer Name]
**Area Path:** Todo-App
**Iteration:** [Next Sprint]
**Tags:** testing, infrastructure, technical-setup

**Description:**
```
As a developer
I want a testing framework configured
So that I can write and run tests

## Context
The application currently has zero test coverage. We need to set up the testing infrastructure before we can write unit tests, integration tests, and E2E tests.
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```
- [ ] Vitest (or Jest) is installed and configured for unit/integration tests
- [ ] Playwright (or Cypress) is installed and configured for E2E tests
- [ ] Testing Library (@testing-library/react) is available for component tests
- [ ] Test scripts are added to package.json (test, test:unit, test:e2e)
- [ ] Tests can run successfully with `npm test`
- [ ] Test configuration files are created and documented
- [ ] Sample test runs successfully to verify setup
- [ ] CI/CD integration is documented (for Story 6.8)
```

**Technical Notes:**
```
Recommended packages:
- vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom
- @playwright/test or cypress
- Configure test scripts in package.json
- Set up test database for integration tests (separate from dev.db)
```

---

### Story 6.2: Write Unit Tests for Server Actions
**Work Item Type:** User Story
**Title:** Write Unit Tests for Server Actions
**State:** New
**Priority:** 1 (Must Have)
**Story Points:** 8
**Assigned To:** [Developer Name]
**Area Path:** Todo-App
**Iteration:** [Next Sprint]
**Tags:** testing, unit-tests, server-actions
**Dependencies:** Story 6.1 (Blocked by)

**Description:**
```
As a developer
I want comprehensive unit tests for server actions
So that I can prevent regressions in core logic

## Context
Server actions in `app/actions/todos.ts` contain all CRUD logic but have no test coverage. These are the highest-risk components and must be tested before production.
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```
- [ ] Tests written for getTodos() covering:
  - Returns sorted todos correctly
  - Handles empty database
  - Handles database errors
- [ ] Tests written for createTodo() covering:
  - Valid input creates todo successfully
  - Missing title returns error
  - Default values applied correctly (priority, completed)
  - Path revalidation called
- [ ] Tests written for updateTodo() covering:
  - Partial updates work correctly

1→# Architecture Review Request: Todo List Application
     2→
     3→**Document Version:** 1.0
     4→**Requested By:** Product Manager (Retroactive Review)
     5→**Date:** 2026-01-15
     6→**Review Type:** POST-IMPLEMENTATION VALIDATION
     7→**Reviewer:** z-cora:system-architect
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Executive Summary
    12→
    13→This document requests a comprehensive architecture review of an already-implemented todo list application. The application was built without formal architectural review, and we need to validate the technical decisions, identify risks, and recommend improvements before moving to production.
    14→
    15→**Key Concern:** Application was built code-first without architectural planning or review.
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Application Overview
    20→
    21→### Purpose
    22→Personal productivity tool enabling users to create, organize, and track daily tasks with priority-based organization and deadline management.
    23→
    24→### Current Status
    25→- Functional implementation complete
    26→- All core features working
    27→- No tests written
    28→- No production deployment yet
    29→
    30→### Users
    31→- Single-user, local-only application
    32→- No authentication required
    33→- Privacy-first (no cloud sync)
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Technology Stack (AS-IMPLEMENTED)
    38→
    39→### Frontend
    40→- **Framework:** Next.js 16.1.2 (App Router)
    41→- **React Version:** 19.2.3
    42→- **Language:** TypeScript 5
    43→- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4
    44→- **Form Library:** React Hook Form 7.71.1
    45→- **Build Optimization:** React Compiler enabled
    46→
    47→### Backend / Data Layer
    48→- **Database:** SQLite (local file)
    49→- **ORM:** Prisma 7.2.0
    50→- **Database Adapter:** @prisma/adapter-libsql 7.2.0
    51→- **Client Library:** @libsql/client 0.17.0
    52→
    53→### Architecture Pattern
    54→- **Pattern:** Next.js App Router with Server Components & Server Actions
    55→- **Data Flow:** Client → Server Actions → Prisma → SQLite
    56→- **State Management:** Server state (via Next.js revalidation), minimal client state
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## Architecture Review Questions
    61→
    62→### 1. Technology Selection Validation
    63→
    64→**Question:** Is Next.js 16 with App Router an appropriate choice for this use case?
    65→- **Rationale for Choice:** Modern framework, SSR support, Server Actions simplify backend
    66→- **Concerns:** Single-user app doesn't need SSR benefits. Possible over-engineering?
    67→- **Alternative Considered:** None (should Vite + React have been considered?)
    68→
    69→**Question:** Is SQLite appropriate for this application?
    70→- **Rationale:** Local-first, zero-configuration, serverless, privacy-focused
    71→- **Concerns:** No multi-device sync, no cloud backup, single-user only
    72→- **Alternative Considered:** None (what about localStorage/IndexedDB for browser-native storage?)
    73→
    74→**Question:** Is Prisma overkill for a simple SQLite database?
    75→- **Rationale:** Type safety, migrations, developer experience
    76→- **Concerns:** Adds build complexity, bundle size, generated client in codebase
    77→- **Alternative Considered:** Could better-sqlite3 or Knex be lighter?
    78→
    79→**Question:** Is libSQL adapter necessary?
    80→- **Rationale:** [Unclear - appears to be default Prisma recommendation]
    81→- **Concerns:** Extra dependency, unclear benefit over native SQLite driver
    82→- **Alternative Considered:** Standard Prisma SQLite setup?
    83→
    84→### 2. Architectural Patterns
    85→
    86→**Question:** Is Server Actions the right pattern for CRUD operations?
    87→- **Current Implementation:** All mutations via Server Actions with `revalidatePath('/')`
    88→- **Benefits:** Simpler than API routes, type-safe, automatic serialization
    89→- **Concerns:** Full page revalidation on every change, no granular updates
    90→- **Alternative:** Should we use React Query/SWR for optimistic updates?
    91→
    92→**Question:** Is the component architecture scalable?
    93→- **Current Structure:**
    94→  - Page (Server Component) → fetches data
    95→  - TodoForm, TodoList, TodoItem (Client Components)
    96→- **Benefits:** Clear separation, React 19 best practices
    97→- **Concerns:** No shared state management, prop drilling potential
    98→- **Alternative:** Context API or Zustand for shared state?
    99→
   100→**Question:** Is data fetching strategy optimal?
   101→- **Current:** Server Component fetches all todos on page load
   102→- **Benefits:** SSR, SEO-friendly (not needed here)
   103→- **Concerns:** No pagination, no infinite scroll, re-fetches everything
   104→- **Alternative:** Client-side data fetching with caching?
   105→
   106→### 3. Data Model Design
   107→
   108→**Question:** Is the Todo schema well-designed?
   109→```prisma
   110→model Todo {
   111→  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
   112→  title       String
   113→  description String?
   114→  completed   Boolean  @default(false)
   115→  priority    String   @default("medium")  // Stored as string
   116→  dueDate     DateTime?
   117→  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
   118→  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
   119→}
   120→```
   121→
   122→**Concerns:**
   123→1. Priority as string instead of enum - no database-level validation
   124→2. No indexes defined - could impact performance with many todos
   125→3. CUID vs UUID vs auto-increment - is CUID the right choice?
   126→4. No soft delete (deletedAt) - permanent deletion only
   127→
   128→**Question:** Should we add database-level constraints?
   129→- Foreign keys (N/A for single table)
   130→- Check constraints (e.g., priority IN ('low','medium','high'))
   131→- Unique constraints (e.g., title uniqueness?)
   132→- Indexes (e.g., on completed, createdAt for sorting)
   133→
   134→### 4. Performance Considerations
   135→
   136→**Question:** Will the application scale to stated requirements?
   137→- **Stated Requirement:** Support 1000+ todos without degradation
   138→- **Current Implementation:** Fetches ALL todos on every page load
   139→- **Concerns:** No pagination, no virtualization, no lazy loading
   140→- **Recommendation Needed:** At what threshold should pagination be added?
   141→
   142→**Question:** Is React Compiler providing value?
   143→- **Current Setting:** `reactCompiler: true` in next.config.ts
   144→- **Question:** Has performance been measured with/without?
   145→- **Recommendation Needed:** Is this premature optimization?
   146→
   147→**Question:** Are there N+1 query risks?
   148→- **Current:** Single `findMany` query for all todos
   149→- **Assessment:** No N+1 risk currently, good
   150→- **Future Risk:** If relationships added (categories, tags), watch for N+1
   151→
   152→### 5. Security & Data Integrity
   153→
   154→**Question:** Are there security vulnerabilities?
   155→- **SQL Injection:** Mitigated by Prisma (parameterized queries)
   156→- **XSS:** Mitigated by React (auto-escaping)
   157→- **CSRF:** Not applicable (no authentication, same-origin only)
   158→- **Input Validation:** Only client-side validation - should add server-side?
   159→
   160→**Question:** Is data integrity protected?
   161→- **Transactions:** Not explicitly used, relying on Prisma defaults
   162→- **Constraint Violations:** No server-side validation of priority values
   163→- **Race Conditions:** Possible on concurrent toggleTodo calls?
   164→- **Data Loss Prevention:** No backup mechanism, no export feature
   165→
   166→### 6. Error Handling & Resilience
   167→
   168→**Question:** Is error handling adequate?
   169→- **Current:** Try-catch blocks return `{success, error}` objects
   170→- **Concerns:**
   171→  - Generic error messages ("Failed to create todo")
   172→  - Errors only logged to console, no monitoring
   173→  - No retry logic for database failures
   174→  - No user-friendly error display (no toast notifications)
   175→
   176→**Question:** What happens when database is locked/unavailable?
   177→- **SQLite Caveat:** Write locks can cause "database is locked" errors
   178→- **Current Handling:** Generic error message
   179→- **Recommendation Needed:** Retry logic? Queue writes?
   180→
   181→### 7. Testing & Quality Assurance
   182→
   183→**Question:** Is the current architecture testable?
   184→- **Server Actions:** Can be tested in isolation ✓
   185→- **Components:** React Testing Library compatible ✓
   186→- **Database:** Can use test database ✓
   187→- **E2E:** Playwright/Cypress compatible ✓
   188→- **Assessment:** Architecture is testable, tests just not written yet
   189→
   190→**Question:** Are there architectural barriers to testing?
   191→- **Prisma Singleton:** Global instance may complicate test isolation
   192→- **Server Actions:** Require Next.js runtime to test (not pure functions)
   193→- **Recommendation Needed:** Testing strategy for server actions?
   194→
   195→### 8. Deployment & Operations
   196→
   197→**Question:** What is the deployment target?
   198→- **Current:** Development only (`npm run dev`)
   199→- **Production Build:** Not tested yet
   200→- **Hosting:** Unknown (Vercel? Self-hosted? Static export?)
   201→- **Database Location:** Local file system (not suitable for serverless)
   202→
   203→**Question:** Is this architecture deployable to serverless?
   204→- **SQLite Limitation:** File-based database not suitable for serverless
   205→- **Options:**
   206→  1. Deploy to long-running server (VPS, container)
   207→  2. Migrate to Turso (serverless SQLite)
   208→  3. Migrate to PostgreSQL/MySQL
   209→- **Recommendation Needed:** Clarify deployment target and adapt architecture
   210→
   211→**Question:** How will database migrations work in production?
   212→- **Current:** `prisma migrate dev` for development
   213→- **Production:** Need `prisma migrate deploy` in deployment pipeline
   214→- **Concerns:** No migration strategy documented
   215→- **Data Loss Risk:** Migrations not tested with production-like data
   216→
   217→### 9. Maintainability & Developer Experience
   218→
   219→**Question:** Is the codebase maintainable?
   220→- **Positive:**
   221→  - TypeScript provides type safety
   222→  - Clear component structure
   223→  - Separation of concerns (UI/Actions/Data)
   224→- **Concerns:**
   225→  - No code documentation
   226→  - No JSDoc comments
   227→  - Magic strings (priority values, paths)
   228→  - Prisma client in generated folder (gitignored?)
   229→
   230→**Question:** Is there technical debt?
   231→- **Identified Debt:**
   232→  1. No tests (critical)
   233→  2. No documentation (high)
   234→  3. No error monitoring (medium)
   235→  4. No logging (medium)
   236→  5. Priority as string not enum (low)
   237→  6. No input sanitization (low)
   238→
   239→### 10. Scalability & Future-Proofing
   240→
   241→**Question:** Can this architecture support v2.0 features?
   242→- **Planned Features (from PRD):**
   243→  - Task categories/projects (requires schema change)
   244→  - Search & filter (requires indexes)
   245→  - Recurring tasks (complex logic)
   246→  - Cloud sync (requires backend rewrite)
   247→  - Multi-user (requires authentication, user_id foreign key)
   248→
   249→**Assessment Needed:** Which features are architectural blockers?
   250→
   251→**Question:** Should we plan for migration path now?
   252→- **From:** SQLite + Server Actions
   253→- **To:** Separate backend (API) + frontend
   254→- **Trigger:** When cloud sync or multi-user needed
   255→- **Recommendation Needed:** Prepare for this now or refactor later?
   256→
   257→---
   258→
   259→## Specific Architecture Decisions to Review
   260→
   261→### Decision 1: Prisma Client Generated Output Location
   262→**Current:** `output = "../app/generated/prisma"`
   263→**Standard:** Usually in `node_modules/.prisma/client`
   264→**Question:** Why was this changed? Is it correct?
   265→**Risk:** Non-standard location may confuse tools or other developers
   266→
   267→### Decision 2: Server Actions vs API Routes
   268→**Current:** All data mutations via Server Actions
   269→**Alternative:** RESTful API routes or tRPC
   270→**Question:** Was this the right choice for this use case?
   271→**Trade-off:** Simpler dev experience vs. flexibility and client independence
   272→
   273→### Decision 3: Full Page Revalidation
   274→**Current:** `revalidatePath('/')` on every mutation
   275→**Impact:** Entire page re-fetches data, re-renders
   276→**Question:** Should we use more granular revalidation or client caching?
   277→**Alternative:** React Query with optimistic updates
   278→
   279→### Decision 4: Client vs Server Components Split
   280→**Current:** Page is Server Component, form/list are Client Components
   281→**Question:** Is this the right boundary?
   282→**Alternative:** Could TodoList be a Server Component with client TodoItems?
   283→
   284→### Decision 5: No Global State Management
   285→**Current:** Each component manages its own state
   286→**Question:** Is this sustainable as complexity grows?
   287→**Concern:** Currently simple, but may need state management for filters, search
   288→
   289→### Decision 6: Form Library Choice
   290→**Current:** React Hook Form
   291→**Alternatives:** Formik, Zod + React 19 actions
   292→**Question:** Is RHF the right choice for this simple form?
   293→**Assessment:** Possibly over-engineered, but provides good DX
   294→
   295→---
   296→
   297→## Architecture Risks Identified
   298→
   299→| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Impact |
   300→|------|----------|------------|--------|
   301→| SQLite file corruption/loss | HIGH | LOW | All user data lost |
   302→| Performance degradation with 1000+ todos | MEDIUM | HIGH | Poor UX, slow operations |
   303→| Database locked errors (SQLite concurrency) | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | Failed operations, user frustration |
   304→| Cannot deploy to serverless (SQLite limitation) | HIGH | HIGH | Deployment options limited |
   305→| No backup/export mechanism | HIGH | MEDIUM | User cannot recover from data loss |

# Architecture Review Request: Todo List Application

**Document Version:** 1.0
**Requested By:** Product Manager (Retroactive Review)
**Date:** 2026-01-15
**Review Type:** POST-IMPLEMENTATION VALIDATION
**Reviewer:** z-cora:system-architect

---

## Executive Summary

This document requests a comprehensive architecture review of an already-implemented todo list application. The application was built without formal architectural review, and we need to validate the technical decisions, identify risks, and recommend improvements before moving to production.

**Key Concern:** Application was built code-first without architectural planning or review.

---

## Application Overview

### Purpose
Personal productivity tool enabling users to create, organize, and track daily tasks with priority-based organization and deadline management.

### Current Status
- Functional implementation complete
- All core features working
- No tests written
- No production deployment yet

### Users
- Single-user, local-only application
- No authentication required
- Privacy-first (no cloud sync)

---

## Technology Stack (AS-IMPLEMENTED)

### Frontend
- **Framework:** Next.js 16.1.2 (App Router)
- **React Version:** 19.2.3
- **Language:** TypeScript 5
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4
- **Form Library:** React Hook Form 7.71.1
- **Build Optimization:** React Compiler enabled

### Backend / Data Layer
- **Database:** SQLite (local file)
- **ORM:** Prisma 7.2.0
- **Database Adapter:** @prisma/adapter-libsql 7.2.0
- **Client Library:** @libsql/client 0.17.0

### Architecture Pattern
- **Pattern:** Next.js App Router with Server Components & Server Actions
- **Data Flow:** Client → Server Actions → Prisma → SQLite
- **State Management:** Server state (via Next.js revalidation), minimal client state

---

## Architecture Review Questions

### 1. Technology Selection Validation

**Question:** Is Next.js 16 with App Router an appropriate choice for this use case?
- **Rationale for Choice:** Modern framework, SSR support, Server Actions simplify backend
- **Concerns:** Single-user app doesn't need SSR benefits. Possible over-engineering?
- **Alternative Considered:** None (should Vite + React have been considered?)

**Question:** Is SQLite appropriate for this application?
- **Rationale:** Local-first, zero-configuration, serverless, privacy-focused
- **Concerns:** No multi-device sync, no cloud backup, single-user only
- **Alternative Considered:** None (what about localStorage/IndexedDB for browser-native storage?)

**Question:** Is Prisma overkill for a simple SQLite database?
- **Rationale:** Type safety, migrations, developer experience
- **Concerns:** Adds build complexity, bundle size, generated client in codebase
- **Alternative Considered:** Could better-sqlite3 or Knex be lighter?

**Question:** Is libSQL adapter necessary?
- **Rationale:** [Unclear - appears to be default Prisma recommendation]
- **Concerns:** Extra dependency, unclear benefit over native SQLite driver
- **Alternative Considered:** Standard Prisma SQLite setup?

### 2. Architectural Patterns

**Question:** Is Server Actions the right pattern for CRUD operations?
- **Current Implementation:** All mutations via Server Actions with `revalidatePath('/')`
- **Benefits:** Simpler than API routes, type-safe, automatic serialization
- **Concerns:** Full page revalidation on every change, no granular updates
- **Alternative:** Should we use React Query/SWR for optimistic updates?

**Question:** Is the component architecture scalable?
- **Current Structure:**
  - Page (Server Component) → fetches data
  - TodoForm, TodoList, TodoItem (Client Components)
- **Benefits:** Clear separation, React 19 best practices
- **Concerns:** No shared state management, prop drilling potential
- **Alternative:** Context API or Zustand for shared state?

**Question:** Is data fetching strategy optimal?
- **Current:** Server Component fetches all todos on page load
- **Benefits:** SSR, SEO-friendly (not needed here)
- **Concerns:** No pagination, no infinite scroll, re-fetches everything
- **Alternative:** Client-side data fetching with caching?

### 3. Data Model Design

**Question:** Is the Todo schema well-designed?
```prisma
model Todo {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  title       String
  description String?
  completed   Boolean  @default(false)
  priority    String   @default("medium")  // Stored as string
  dueDate     DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
}
```

**Concerns:**
1. Priority as string instead of enum - no database-level validation
2. No indexes defined - could impact performance with many todos
3. CUID vs UUID vs auto-increment - is CUID the right choice?
4. No soft delete (deletedAt) - permanent deletion only

**Question:** Should we add database-level constraints?
- Foreign keys (N/A for single table)
- Check constraints (e.g., priority IN ('low','medium','high'))
- Unique constraints (e.g., title uniqueness?)
- Indexes (e.g., on completed, createdAt for sorting)

### 4. Performance Considerations

**Question:** Will the application scale to stated requirements?
- **Stated Requirement:** Support 1000+ todos without degradation
- **Current Implementation:** Fetches ALL todos on every page load
- **Concerns:** No pagination, no virtualization, no lazy loading
- **Recommendation Needed:** At what threshold should pagination be added?

**Question:** Is React Compiler providing value?
- **Current Setting:** `reactCompiler: true` in next.config.ts
- **Question:** Has performance been measured with/without?
- **Recommendation Needed:** Is this premature optimization?

**Question:** Are there N+1 query risks?
- **Current:** Single `findMany` query for all todos
- **Assessment:** No N+1 risk currently, good
- **Future Risk:** If relationships added (categories, tags), watch for N+1

### 5. Security & Data Integrity

**Question:** Are there security vulnerabilities?
- **SQL Injection:** Mitigated by Prisma (parameterized queries)
- **XSS:** Mitigated by React (auto-escaping)
- **CSRF:** Not applicable (no authentication, same-origin only)
- **Input Validation:** Only client-side validation - should add server-side?

**Question:** Is data integrity protected?
- **Transactions:** Not explicitly used, relying on Prisma defaults
- **Constraint Violations:** No server-side validation of priority values
- **Race Conditions:** Possible on concurrent toggleTodo calls?
- **Data Loss Prevention:** No backup mechanism, no export feature

### 6. Error Handling & Resilience

**Question:** Is error handling adequate?
- **Current:** Try-catch blocks return `{success, error}` objects
- **Concerns:**
  - Generic error messages ("Failed to create todo")
  - Errors only logged to console, no monitoring
  - No retry logic for database failures
  - No user-friendly error display (no toast notifications)

**Question:** What happens when database is locked/unavailable?
- **SQLite Caveat:** Write locks can cause "database is locked" errors
- **Current Handling:** Generic error message
- **Recommendation Needed:** Retry logic? Queue writes?

### 7. Testing & Quality Assurance

**Question:** Is the current architecture testable?
- **Server Actions:** Can be tested in isolation ✓
- **Components:** React Testing Library compatible ✓
- **Database:** Can use test database ✓
- **E2E:** Playwright/Cypress compatible ✓
- **Assessment:** Architecture is testable, tests just not written yet

**Question:** Are there architectural barriers to testing?
- **Prisma Singleton:** Global instance may complicate test isolation
- **Server Actions:** Require Next.js runtime to test (not pure functions)
- **Recommendation Needed:** Testing strategy for server actions?

### 8. Deployment & Operations

**Question:** What is the deployment target?
- **Current:** Development only (`npm run dev`)
- **Production Build:** Not tested yet
- **Hosting:** Unknown (Vercel? Self-hosted? Static export?)
- **Database Location:** Local file system (not suitable for serverless)

**Question:** Is this architecture deployable to serverless?
- **SQLite Limitation:** File-based database not suitable for serverless
- **Options:**
  1. Deploy to long-running server (VPS, container)
  2. Migrate to Turso (serverless SQLite)
  3. Migrate to PostgreSQL/MySQL
- **Recommendation Needed:** Clarify deployment target and adapt architecture

**Question:** How will database migrations work in production?
- **Current:** `prisma migrate dev` for development
- **Production:** Need `prisma migrate deploy` in deployment pipeline
- **Concerns:** No migration strategy documented
- **Data Loss Risk:** Migrations not tested with production-like data

### 9. Maintainability & Developer Experience

**Question:** Is the codebase maintainable?
- **Positive:**
  - TypeScript provides type safety
  - Clear component structure
  - Separation of concerns (UI/Actions/Data)
- **Concerns:**
  - No code documentation
  - No JSDoc comments
  - Magic strings (priority values, paths)
  - Prisma client in generated folder (gitignored?)

**Question:** Is there technical debt?
- **Identified Debt:**
  1. No tests (critical)
  2. No documentation (high)
  3. No error monitoring (medium)
  4. No logging (medium)
  5. Priority as string not enum (low)
  6. No input sanitization (low)

### 10. Scalability & Future-Proofing

**Question:** Can this architecture support v2.0 features?
- **Planned Features (from PRD):**
  - Task categories/projects (requires schema change)
  - Search & filter (requires indexes)
  - Recurring tasks (complex logic)
  - Cloud sync (requires backend rewrite)
  - Multi-user (requires authentication, user_id foreign key)

**Assessment Needed:** Which features are architectural blockers?

**Question:** Should we plan for migration path now?
- **From:** SQLite + Server Actions
- **To:** Separate backend (API) + frontend
- **Trigger:** When cloud sync or multi-user needed
- **Recommendation Needed:** Prepare for this now or refactor later?

---

## Specific Architecture Decisions to Review

### Decision 1: Prisma Client Generated Output Location
**Current:** `output = "../app/generated/prisma"`
**Standard:** Usually in `node_modules/.prisma/client`
**Question:** Why was this changed? Is it correct?
**Risk:** Non-standard location may confuse tools or other developers

### Decision 2: Server Actions vs API Routes
**Current:** All data mutations via Server Actions
**Alternative:** RESTful API routes or tRPC
**Question:** Was this the right choice for this use case?
**Trade-off:** Simpler dev experience vs. flexibility and client independence

### Decision 3: Full Page Revalidation
**Current:** `revalidatePath('/')` on every mutation
**Impact:** Entire page re-fetches data, re-renders
**Question:** Should we use more granular revalidation or client caching?
**Alternative:** React Query with optimistic updates

### Decision 4: Client vs Server Components Split
**Current:** Page is Server Component, form/list are Client Components
**Question:** Is this the right boundary?
**Alternative:** Could TodoList be a Server Component with client TodoItems?

### Decision 5: No Global State Management
**Current:** Each component manages its own state
**Question:** Is this sustainable as complexity grows?
**Concern:** Currently simple, but may need state management for filters, search

### Decision 6: Form Library Choice
**Current:** React Hook Form
**Alternatives:** Formik, Zod + React 19 actions
**Question:** Is RHF the right choice for this simple form?
**Assessment:** Possibly over-engineered, but provides good DX

---

## Architecture Risks Identified

| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Impact |
|------|----------|------------|--------|
| SQLite file corruption/loss | HIGH | LOW | All user data lost |
| Performance degradation with 1000+ todos | MEDIUM | HIGH | Poor UX, slow operations |
| Database locked errors (SQLite concurrency) | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | Failed operations, user frustration |
| Cannot deploy to serverless (SQLite limitation) | HIGH | HIGH | Deployment options limited |
| No backup/export mechanism | HIGH | MEDIUM | User cannot recover from data loss |
| Server-side validation missing | MEDIUM | LOW | Invalid data in database |
| No monitoring/observability | MEDIUM | HIGH | Cannot detect production issues |
| Architecture doesn't support v2.0 features | HIGH | MEDIUM | Major refactor needed for growth |

---

## Architecture Review Deliverables Requested

Please provide:

1. **Architecture Assessment Report**
   - Overall architecture quality rating
   - Strengths and weaknesses
   - Comparison to best practices

2. **Technology Stack Validation**
   - Assess each technology choice
   - Recommend keep/replace/modify for each
   - Provide rationale

3. **Architecture Diagram**
   - Current system architecture
   - Data flow diagram
   - Component hierarchy
   - Deployment architecture (if applicable)

4. **Risk Analysis**
   - Expand on risks identified above
   - Add any risks missed
   - Prioritize risks by severity × likelihood
   - Recommend mitigations

5. **Technical Debt Report**
   - Catalog all technical debt
   - Estimate effort to address each item
   - Prioritize by risk and effort

6. **Refactoring Recommendations**
   - What should be refactored immediately
   - What can wait for v2.0
   - What is fine as-is

7. **Production Readiness Checklist**
   - What must be done before production
   - What deployment options are viable
   - Infrastructure requirements

8. **Future-Proofing Assessment**
   - Can this architecture support PRD v2.0 features?
   - What would need to change?
   - Should we refactor preemptively?

---

## Files for Review

Please examine these files in detail:

### Core Implementation
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/app/actions/todos.ts` - Server actions (CRUD logic)
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/app/page.tsx` - Main page (Server Component)
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/app/components/TodoForm.tsx` - Form component
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/app/components/TodoItem.tsx` - Item display
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/app/components/TodoList.tsx` - List container

### Data Layer
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/prisma/schema.prisma` - Database schema
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/lib/prisma.ts` - Prisma client config

### Configuration
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/package.json` - Dependencies
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/next.config.ts` - Next.js config
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/tsconfig.json` - TypeScript config

### Documentation (Created Retroactively)
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/docs/PRD-TodoApp.md` - Product requirements
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/docs/EPICS-TodoApp.md` - Epic breakdown
- `/Users/tadeumarques/Coding/tadeu-todo-app/docs/USER-STORIES-TodoApp.md` - User stories

---

## Review Context

### What Went Wrong
- Application was built without architectural planning
- No design review or technical spike
- No consideration of deployment target
- Technology choices not validated
- No performance testing or sizing
- No security review

### What We Need Now

### Success Criteria for This Review

- Confidence that we understand what we built

---

## Timeline

# Product Management Assessment & Recommendations
## Todo List Application - Retroactive Review

**Document Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-01-15
**Prepared By:** Product Manager (z-cora)
**Project:** Todo List Application
**Status:** POST-IMPLEMENTATION REVIEW

---

## Executive Summary

This document provides a comprehensive product management assessment of a todo list application that was developed **without following proper product management processes**. This is a retroactive analysis to document what should have been done and to chart a path forward to production quality.

### Key Findings

**What Went Well:**
- Core functionality is complete and working
- Modern, accessible tech stack
- Clean, maintainable code structure
- User experience is intuitive

**Critical Gaps:**
- No PRD or requirements documentation
- No user research or validation
- No test coverage (0%)
- No architectural review
- No production deployment plan
- No success metrics defined

**Recommendation:** Do NOT deploy to production until critical gaps are addressed (estimated 3-4 weeks of work).

---

## What Should Have Happened (Ideal Process)

### Phase 1: Discovery (Week 1-2)
1. **User Research**
   - Conduct 5-10 user interviews with target audience
   - Identify pain points with existing todo solutions
   - Calculate opportunity scores
   - Make go/no-go decision

2. **Market Analysis**
   - Competitive analysis of existing solutions
   - Feature gap analysis
   - Value proposition definition

3. **Success Metrics**
   - Define key metrics (adoption, retention, satisfaction)
   - Baseline current state (if replacing existing solution)

**Deliverable:** Opportunity Assessment Document with go/no-go recommendation

### Phase 2: Requirements (Week 2-3)
1. **PRD Creation**
   - Problem statement
   - User personas and journeys
   - Functional requirements
   - Non-functional requirements (performance, accessibility, security)
   - Success criteria

2. **Stakeholder Alignment**
   - Review PRD with stakeholders
   - Get approval on scope and priorities
   - Confirm resource allocation

**Deliverable:** Approved PRD

### Phase 3: Architecture & Design (Week 3-4)
1. **Technical Spike**
   - Technology evaluation
   - Architecture design
   - Database schema design
   - API contract definition

2. **Architecture Review**
   - System architect reviews design
   - Identifies risks and trade-offs
   - Validates technology choices

3. **UX Design** (if designer available)
   - Wireframes
   - Mockups
   - User flow diagrams

**Deliverable:** Technical Design Document, Architecture Approval

### Phase 4: Planning (Week 4-5)
1. **Epic Breakdown**
   - Break PRD into epics
   - Estimate effort
   - Map dependencies

2. **Story Writing**
   - Write detailed user stories with acceptance criteria
   - Size stories (story points)
   - Prioritize (MoSCoW or RICE)

3. **Sprint Planning**
   - Create sprints
   - Assign stories to sprints
   - Load balance team capacity

**Deliverable:** Backlog in Azure DevOps, Sprint Plan

### Phase 5: Development (Week 5-11)
1. **Sprint Execution**
   - Daily standups
   - Story completion with tests
   - Code reviews
   - Continuous integration

2. **PM Activities**
   - Unblock developers
   - Refine upcoming stories
   - Demo completed work
   - Adjust priorities as needed

**Deliverable:** Working software with tests

### Phase 6: Quality Assurance (Week 11-12)
1. **Testing**
   - Unit tests (during development)
   - Integration tests
   - E2E tests
   - Accessibility audit
   - Performance testing
   - Security review

2. **Bug Fixing**
   - Triage bugs by severity
   - Fix critical/high bugs
   - Defer low-priority bugs to backlog

**Deliverable:** Production-ready application

### Phase 7: Launch (Week 13)
1. **Pre-Launch**
   - Production environment setup
   - Deployment runbook
   - Rollback plan
   - Monitoring setup

2. **Launch**
   - Deploy to production
   - Monitor for errors
   - Collect user feedback

3. **Post-Launch**
   - Measure success metrics
   - Iterate based on feedback
   - Plan v2.0

**Deliverable:** Live application with monitoring

**Total Timeline:** 13 weeks (3 months) for proper process

---

## What Actually Happened

### Reality (Estimated 2 weeks)
1. ~~Discovery~~ **Skipped** - No user research
2. ~~Requirements~~ **Skipped** - No PRD written
3. ~~Architecture Review~~ **Skipped** - No architectural planning
4. ~~Story Writing~~ **Skipped** - No formal stories
5. **Development** - Code written directly based on mental model
6. ~~Testing~~ **Skipped** - No tests written
7. ~~Launch Prep~~ **Skipped** - Still in development

### Result
- **Velocity:** Very fast (2 weeks vs 13 weeks ideal)
- **Quality:** Unknown (no tests, no validation)
- **Risk:** High (no architectural review, no security review)
- **Maintainability:** Uncertain (no documentation)
- **Production Readiness:** Not ready (critical gaps)

---

## Gap Analysis

### Documentation Gaps

| Required Document | Status | Risk Level | Effort to Create |
|------------------|--------|------------|------------------|
| PRD | Missing → Created | HIGH | 8 hours → DONE |
| Epic Breakdown | Missing → Created | HIGH | 4 hours → DONE |
| User Stories | Missing → Created | HIGH | 8 hours → DONE |
| Architecture Docs | Missing → Requested | HIGH | 16 hours |
| Technical Design | Missing | MEDIUM | 8 hours |
| API Documentation | N/A (Server Actions) | LOW | N/A |
| User Guide | Missing | LOW | 4 hours |
| Deployment Guide | Missing | HIGH | 4 hours |

**Total Documentation Debt:** ~52 hours (1.5 weeks)

### Testing Gaps

| Test Type | Current Coverage | Target Coverage | Effort to Achieve |
|-----------|-----------------|-----------------|-------------------|
| Unit Tests | 0% | 80%+ | 24 hours |
| Integration Tests | 0% | Critical paths | 16 hours |
| E2E Tests | 0% | Critical flows | 16 hours |
| Accessibility Tests | 0% | WCAG AA | 8 hours |

## Long-Term Recommendations

### Process Improvements
1. **Implement PM Workflow**
   - All future features go through Discovery → Requirements → Design → Development
   - No coding without approved stories
   - Regular sprint reviews and retrospectives

2. **Establish Quality Gates**
   - PRD must be approved before epics created
   - Epics must be approved before stories created
   - Stories must have tests before marked done
   - Features must pass QA before deployed

3. **User Research Cadence**
   - Monthly user interviews (even if small scale)
   - Track satisfaction metrics
   - Maintain feedback backlog

### Technical Improvements
1. **Testing Standards**
   - Minimum 80% code coverage required
   - E2E tests for all critical flows
   - Accessibility tests in CI/CD
   - Performance budgets enforced

2. **Documentation Standards**
   - JSDoc comments on all functions
   - README with setup instructions
   - Architecture decision records (ADRs)
   - API documentation (even for Server Actions)

3. **Code Quality**
   - Required code reviews
   - Linting rules enforced
   - TypeScript strict mode
   - Automated formatting (Prettier)

### Product Improvements
1. **Metrics & Analytics**
   - Implement basic usage tracking
   - Track task completion rates
   - Monitor performance metrics
   - User feedback mechanism

2. **V2.0 Planning**
   - Prioritize v2.0 features based on user feedback
   - Consider cloud sync and multi-device support
   - Evaluate mobile app viability
   - Plan for scalability

---

## Lessons Learned

### What to Do Differently Next Time

**1. Start with User Research**
- Even a simple app needs validation
- 5-10 interviews can prevent major missteps
- Understand the problem before jumping to solutions

**2. Write PRD Before Coding**
- Forces clarity on requirements
- Prevents scope creep
- Creates shared understanding
- Documents decisions for future reference

**3. Architecture Review Early**
- Technology choices have long-term impacts
- Architect can identify risks early
- Cheaper to refactor design than code

**4. Write Tests During Development**
- Test-after is harder than test-during
- Tests guide better design
- Prevents regression
- Builds confidence

**5. Define Success Metrics**
- Can't improve what you don't measure
- Metrics drive prioritization
- Validate assumptions with data

**6. Document as You Go**
- Documentation after the fact is incomplete
- Decisions get forgotten
- Future maintainers struggle

### What Worked Well Despite Process Gaps

**1. Modern Tech Stack**
- Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript are solid choices
- Developer experience is good
- Code is maintainable

**2. Clean Code Structure**
- Clear separation of concerns
- Components are well-organized
- Easy to understand and modify

**3. User-Focused Design**
- Intuitive interface
- Accessibility considered (color contrast, semantic HTML)
- Responsive design

**4. Scope Discipline**
- Didn't over-engineer
- Focused on core features
- Avoided feature creep (so far)

# User Stories: Todo List Application

**Document Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-15
**Status:** RETROACTIVE (Post-Implementation Documentation)
**Epic Reference:** `docs/EPICS-TodoApp.md`

---

## Story Format & Standards

All stories follow the INVEST criteria:
- **I**ndependent: Can be developed and delivered independently
- **N**egotiable: Details can be refined during implementation
- **V**aluable: Delivers clear user value
- **E**stimable: Can be sized with reasonable accuracy
- **S**mall: Completable within one sprint
- **T**estable: Has clear acceptance criteria

**Story Template:**
```
Title: [Action-oriented description]
As a [user type]
I want to [action]
So that [benefit]

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given [context] When [action] Then [outcome]

Story Points: [1, 2, 3, 5, 8]
Priority: [Must/Should/Could/Won't]
Status: [Completed/In Progress/Not Started]
```

---

# Epic 1: Core Data Infrastructure

## Story 1.1: Define Todo Database Schema
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a developer
I want to define a database schema for todos
So that we have a structured, type-safe data model

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the application needs to store todos
- When I define the Prisma schema
- Then the Todo model includes:
  - id (String, CUID, primary key)
  - title (String, required)
  - description (String, optional)
  - completed (Boolean, default false)
  - priority (String, default "medium")
  - dueDate (DateTime, optional)
  - createdAt (DateTime, auto-generated)
  - updatedAt (DateTime, auto-updated)
- And the schema uses SQLite as datasource
- And the Prisma client generates to correct output directory

### Technical Notes
- Use CUID for IDs (collision-resistant)
- Priority stored as string (validated at application layer)
- Timestamps for audit trail

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Schema file created at `prisma/schema.prisma`
- [x] All required fields defined
- [x] Migrations generated and applied
- [ ] Schema documented

---

## Story 1.2: Configure Prisma Client with libSQL Adapter
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a developer
I want to configure Prisma with the libSQL adapter
So that we have efficient SQLite database operations

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the Prisma schema is defined
- When I configure the Prisma client
- Then it uses the PrismaLibSql adapter
- And it connects to the local SQLite database
- And it implements singleton pattern for Next.js hot reload
- And it handles both development and production environments

### Technical Notes
- Use environment variable for DATABASE_URL
- Fallback to `file:./dev.db` in development
- Prevent multiple client instances in dev mode

### Definition of Done
- [x] Prisma client configured at `lib/prisma.ts`
- [x] Singleton pattern implemented
- [x] Environment variable support added
- [ ] Configuration documented

---

## Story 1.3: Implement Server Action for Creating Todos
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a developer
I want to implement a server action for creating todos
So that users can add new tasks to the database

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given a user provides todo data
- When they submit the create todo action
- Then a new todo is created in the database
- And the todo includes all provided fields (title, description, priority, dueDate)
- And default values are applied for missing optional fields
- And the page is revalidated to show the new todo
- And errors are caught and returned with appropriate messages

### Technical Notes
- Use `'use server'` directive
- Type-safe input with TypeScript interface
- Call `revalidatePath('/')` after successful creation
- Return success/error response object

### Definition of Done
- [x] `createTodo()` server action implemented
- [x] Input types defined (CreateTodoInput)
- [x] Error handling implemented
- [x] Path revalidation added
- [ ] Unit tests written

---

## Story 1.4: Implement Server Actions for Reading Todos
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to see all my todos when I open the app
So that I can review my tasks

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have todos in the database
- When I open the application
- Then all todos are fetched from the database
- And they are ordered by completion status (active first)
- And within each status, they are sorted by creation date (newest first)
- And the todos are displayed immediately (SSR)

### Technical Notes
- Use Prisma `findMany` with `orderBy`
- Fetch on server (Server Component)
- Handle empty state gracefully

### Definition of Done
- [x] `getTodos()` server action implemented
- [x] Sorting logic correct (completed asc, createdAt desc)
- [x] Error handling implemented
- [x] Used in server component (page.tsx)
- [ ] Unit tests written

---

## Story 1.5: Implement Server Action for Updating Todos
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to update any field of my todo
So that I can modify tasks as my needs change

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have an existing todo
- When I update any field (title, description, priority, dueDate, completed)
- Then only the provided fields are updated
- And the updatedAt timestamp is automatically set
- And the page is revalidated to reflect changes
- And errors are handled gracefully

### Technical Notes
- Partial update support (don't require all fields)
- Use Prisma `update` with `where` clause
- UpdateTodoInput type allows optional fields

### Definition of Done
- [x] `updateTodo()` server action implemented
- [x] Partial update support working
- [x] Path revalidation added
- [x] Error handling implemented
- [ ] Unit tests written

---

## Story 1.6: Implement Server Action for Deleting Todos
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to delete todos I no longer need
So that I can keep my task list clean

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have an existing todo
- When I delete it
- Then it is permanently removed from the database
- And the page is revalidated to hide the deleted todo
- And I receive confirmation that deletion succeeded

### Technical Notes
- Confirmation dialog handled on client side
- Server action performs actual deletion
- Handle case where todo doesn't exist

### Definition of Done
- [x] `deleteTodo()` server action implemented
- [x] Path revalidation added
- [x] Error handling for non-existent todos
- [ ] Unit tests written

---

## Story 1.7: Implement Server Action for Toggling Todo Completion
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to quickly mark todos as complete or incomplete
So that I can track my progress

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have a todo with any completion status
- When I toggle it
- Then its completion status flips (complete ↔ incomplete)
- And the page is revalidated to show the new state
- And the todo moves to the appropriate section

### Technical Notes
- Fetch current state first
- Flip the boolean value
- Could be optimized with direct SQL UPDATE

### Definition of Done
- [x] `toggleTodo()` server action implemented
- [x] Reads current state before toggling
- [x] Path revalidation added
- [x] Error handling implemented
- [ ] Unit tests written

---

# Epic 2: Task Creation & Management

## Story 2.1: Create Todo Form Component
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want a form to create new todos
So that I can quickly capture tasks

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I am on the home page
- When I see the form
- Then it includes fields for:
  - Title (required text input)
  - Description (optional textarea)
  - Priority (select dropdown with low/medium/high)
  - Due Date (optional date picker)
- And the title field is marked as required
- And the form has a submit button labeled "Add Todo"
- And the form uses proper labels for accessibility

### Technical Notes
- Use React Hook Form for form state
- Client component ('use client')
- Professional styling with Tailwind

### Definition of Done
- [x] TodoForm component created
- [x] All fields implemented with proper inputs
- [x] Form labels associated with inputs
- [x] Styling applied
- [ ] Component tests written

---

## Story 2.2: Implement Form Validation
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want the form to validate my input
So that I can't submit invalid todos

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I interact with the form
- When I try to submit without a title
- Then I see an error message "Title is required"
- And the form does not submit
- And the submit button remains clickable after error
- When I provide a valid title
- Then the error message disappears
- And I can submit the form

### Technical Notes
- Use React Hook Form validation
- Display errors inline below fields
- Error messages in red text

### Definition of Done
- [x] Title validation implemented
- [x] Error messages display correctly
- [x] Errors clear when input is valid
- [ ] Validation tests written

---

## Story 2.3: Implement Form Submission with Loading State
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want immediate feedback when I submit the form
So that I know my action is being processed

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have filled out the form
- When I click "Add Todo"
- Then the button text changes to "Adding..."
- And the button is disabled during submission
- And after successful submission, the form resets
- And the button returns to "Add Todo" state
- And the button is re-enabled

### Technical Notes
- Use useState for loading state
- Disable button when isLoading is true
- Call form reset() after success

### Definition of Done
- [x] Loading state implemented
- [x] Button disabled during submission
- [x] Form resets after success
- [x] Button text changes during loading
- [ ] Integration tests written

---

## Story 2.4: Integrate Form with Create Server Action
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want my submitted form data to create a todo in the database
So that my tasks are saved

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I submit a valid form
- When the form submission completes
- Then createTodo server action is called with form data
- And the new todo appears in the todo list immediately
- And the form is cleared for the next entry

### Technical Notes
- Call createTodo from form onSubmit handler
- Transform form data to match CreateTodoInput type
- Convert date string to Date object

### Definition of Done
- [x] Form calls createTodo server action
- [x] Data transformation correct
- [x] New todo appears in list
- [ ] E2E test for create flow

---

## Story 2.5: Implement Delete Todo Functionality
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to delete todos I no longer need
So that I can keep my list clean and focused

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have a todo displayed
- When I click the "Delete" button
- Then I see a confirmation dialog "Are you sure you want to delete this todo?"
- When I confirm
- Then the todo is deleted from the database
- And it disappears from the list immediately
- When I cancel
- Then the todo remains in the list

### Technical Notes
- Use browser confirm() for simplicity
- Call deleteTodo server action
- Show loading state during deletion

### Definition of Done
- [x] Delete button added to TodoItem
- [x] Confirmation dialog implemented
- [x] Todo deleted on confirmation
- [x] Loading state during deletion
- [ ] E2E test for delete flow

---

# Epic 3: Task Display & Organization

## Story 3.1: Create TodoList Container Component
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to see my todos organized in a clear list
So that I can easily scan my tasks

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have todos in the database
- When I view the application
- Then todos are displayed in two sections:
  - "Active Tasks" section for incomplete todos
  - "Completed" section for completed todos
- And each section has a clear header
- And sections only appear if they contain todos
- And an empty state appears if there are no todos at all

### Technical Notes
- Filter todos by completed property
- Client component for interactivity
- Conditional section rendering

### Definition of Done
- [x] TodoList component created
- [x] Active/Completed filtering logic
- [x] Section headers rendered
- [x] Empty state message
- [ ] Component tests written

---

## Story 3.2: Create TodoItem Display Component
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want each todo to display all its information
So that I can see task details at a glance

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given a todo is displayed
- When I view it
- Then I see:
  - A checkbox indicating completion status
  - The todo title (bold)
  - The description (if provided)
  - A priority badge with color coding
  - The due date (if provided) in format "Due: Mon DD, YYYY"
  - A delete button
- And completed todos have strikethrough text
- And completed todos have muted colors

### Technical Notes
- Map priority to badge colors (low=blue, medium=amber, high=red)
- Format dates with toLocaleDateString
- Apply conditional styling based on completed status

### Definition of Done
- [x] TodoItem component created
- [x] All fields displayed correctly
- [x] Priority badges color-coded
- [x] Date formatting implemented
- [x] Completed styling applied
- [ ] Component tests written

---

## Story 3.3: Implement Priority Badge Color Coding
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to quickly identify task priority
So that I can focus on important work

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given a todo has a priority level
- When I view it
- Then the priority badge is colored:
  - Low: Blue background with blue text
  - Medium: Amber/yellow background with amber text
  - High: Red background with red text
- And the badge is small and rounded
- And the text is uppercase for consistency

### Technical Notes
- Use Tailwind utility classes for colors
- WCAG AA compliant contrast ratios
- Priority stored as string in database

### Definition of Done
- [x] Priority color mapping implemented
- [x] Badge styling applied
- [x] Contrast ratios verified
- [ ] Visual regression tests

---

## Story 3.4: Implement Responsive Layout
**Priority:** SHOULD HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want the app to work on my phone and computer
So that I can manage tasks on any device

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I access the app on different devices
- When viewport is < 768px (mobile)
- Then the form and list stack vertically
- When viewport is ≥ 768px (desktop)
- Then form and list appear side-by-side in two columns
- And both layouts are fully functional
- And text remains readable at all sizes
- And interactive elements are touch-friendly on mobile

### Technical Notes
- Use Tailwind responsive breakpoints (md:)
- Grid layout for desktop, stack for mobile
- Minimum tap target size 44x44px

### Definition of Done
- [x] Mobile layout (320px+) functional
- [x] Desktop layout (768px+) functional
- [x] Breakpoint at md (768px)
- [x] Touch targets sized appropriately
- [ ] Responsive tests written

---

# Epic 4: Task Completion Workflow

## Story 4.1: Implement Checkbox Toggle for Completion
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want to check off completed tasks
So that I can track my progress

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have a todo displayed
- When I click the checkbox
- Then the todo completion status toggles
- And the UI updates immediately
- And the checkbox is disabled during the server update
- And the todo moves to the appropriate section (active/completed)

### Technical Notes
- Call toggleTodo server action
- Disable checkbox while loading
- Next.js will re-render with new data after revalidation

### Definition of Done
- [x] Checkbox input added to TodoItem
- [x] onChange handler calls toggleTodo
- [x] Loading state prevents double-clicks
- [x] Checkbox reflects completion status
- [ ] E2E test for toggle flow

---

## Story 4.2: Apply Visual Styling for Completed Tasks
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want completed tasks to look different
So that I can focus on active work

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given a todo is marked complete
- When I view it
- Then the title has strikethrough text
- And the text color is muted (gray)
- And the background is slightly faded
- And the hover effect is less prominent
- When I unmark it as complete
- Then it returns to normal styling

### Technical Notes
- Conditional classes based on completed prop
- line-through decoration for title
- Gray colors for completed items

### Definition of Done
- [x] Strikethrough applied to completed todos
- [x] Muted colors for completed state
- [x] Styling toggles with completion status
- [ ] Visual regression tests

---

## Story 4.3: Auto-Sort Completed Tasks to Bottom
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 1
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want completed tasks to move to the bottom
So that active tasks stay at the top of my focus

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have both active and completed todos
- When I view my task list
- Then active tasks appear in the "Active Tasks" section at the top
- And completed tasks appear in the "Completed" section below
- And within each section, tasks are sorted by creation date (newest first)

### Technical Notes
- Sorting happens in getTodos server action
- Order by completed (asc), then createdAt (desc)
- TodoList component splits into sections

### Definition of Done
- [x] Server action sorts correctly
- [x] Sections display in correct order
- [x] Within-section sorting correct
- [ ] Sorting tests written

---

# Epic 5: UI/UX Polish & Accessibility

## Story 5.1: Implement Professional Color Palette
**Priority:** SHOULD HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want the app to look professional
So that I enjoy using it

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I view the application
- When I see the interface
- Then it uses a cohesive color palette:
  - Gradient background (blue to indigo)
  - White card backgrounds
  - Blue primary actions
  - Gray text with proper hierarchy
  - Color-coded priority badges
- And all colors meet WCAG AA contrast requirements

### Technical Notes
- Use Tailwind color utilities
- Gradient on body/main container
- Card shadows for depth

### Definition of Done
- [x] Color palette applied throughout
- [x] Gradient background implemented
- [x] Contrast ratios verified (WCAG AA)
- [ ] Accessibility audit passed

---

## Story 5.2: Implement Keyboard Navigation
**Priority:** MUST HAVE (for accessibility)
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** COMPLETED (needs verification)

As a keyboard user
I want to navigate the entire app with keyboard only
So that I can use it without a mouse

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I am using only keyboard
- When I press Tab
- Then focus moves through all interactive elements in logical order:
  1. Title input
  2. Description textarea
  3. Priority select
  4. Due date input
  5. Submit button
  6. Each todo's checkbox
  7. Each todo's delete button
- And focused elements have visible focus indicators
- And I can activate buttons with Enter or Space
- And I can check checkboxes with Space

### Technical Notes
- Browser default tab order should work
- Ensure focus styles are visible (ring utilities)
- Test with keyboard only

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Tab order verified
- [x] Focus indicators visible
- [ ] All interactions keyboard accessible
- [ ] Keyboard navigation tested

---

## Story 5.3: Add Focus Indicators for Accessibility
**Priority:** MUST HAVE (for accessibility)
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a keyboard or screen reader user
I want clear visual focus indicators
So that I know where I am in the interface

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I navigate with keyboard
- When an element receives focus
- Then it displays a visible focus ring
- And the ring color is the primary blue
- And the ring is visible against all backgrounds
- And the ring thickness is at least 2px

### Technical Notes
- Tailwind focus: utilities
- focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500
- focus:ring-offset-2 for separation

### Definition of Done
- [x] All form inputs have focus rings
- [x] All buttons have focus rings
- [x] Checkboxes have focus indicators
- [ ] Manual keyboard test passed

---

## Story 5.4: Implement Smooth Transitions and Animations
**Priority:** COULD HAVE
**Story Points:** 2
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a user
I want smooth visual feedback
So that the interface feels polished

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I interact with the interface
- When state changes occur
- Then transitions are smooth and subtle:
  - Button hover effects fade in/out
  - Card shadows grow on hover
  - Checkbox changes smoothly
- And animations run at 60fps
- And animations respect user's motion preferences

### Technical Notes
- Tailwind transition utilities
- transition-colors, transition-shadow
- respect prefers-reduced-motion

### Definition of Done
- [x] Hover transitions added to buttons
- [x] Card hover effects implemented
- [x] Transitions smooth (60fps)
- [ ] Reduced motion preference handled

---

## Story 5.5: Implement Empty State Message
**Priority:** SHOULD HAVE
**Story Points:** 1
**Status:** COMPLETED

As a new user
I want guidance when I have no todos
So that I know what to do next

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given I have no todos in the database
- When I view the todo list
- Then I see a message: "No todos yet. Create your first one!"
- And the message is centered in the list area
- And the message has adequate padding and readable text

### Technical Notes
- Conditional render in TodoList component
- Only show when todos.length === 0

### Definition of Done
- [x] Empty state component created
- [x] Message displays when no todos
- [x] Styling applied
- [ ] Empty state test written

---

# Epic 6: Quality Assurance & Testing (FUTURE - NOT IMPLEMENTED)

## Story 6.1: Set Up Testing Infrastructure
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want a testing framework configured
So that I can write and run tests

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the application needs tests
- When I set up testing infrastructure
- Then Vitest (or Jest) is configured for unit/integration tests
- And Playwright (or Cypress) is configured for E2E tests
- And Testing Library is available for component tests
- And tests can run with `npm test`
- And tests run in CI/CD pipeline

### Technical Notes
- Install vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom
- Install @playwright/test or cypress
- Configure test scripts in package.json
- Set up test database for integration tests

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Test frameworks installed
- [ ] Configuration files created
- [ ] Sample test runs successfully
- [ ] CI/CD integration documented

---

## Story 6.2: Write Unit Tests for Server Actions
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 8
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want comprehensive unit tests for server actions
So that I can prevent regressions in core logic

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given each server action (getTodos, createTodo, updateTodo, deleteTodo, toggleTodo)
- When I run unit tests
- Then each action has tests covering:
  - Success path
  - Error handling
  - Edge cases (empty data, invalid IDs, etc.)
  - Input validation
- And test coverage is > 90% for server actions

### Technical Notes
- Mock Prisma client for isolation
- Use test database or mocked responses
- Test both success and error returns

### Test Cases to Implement
- `createTodo`: Valid input creates todo, missing title returns error
- `getTodos`: Returns sorted todos, handles empty database
- `updateTodo`: Partial updates work, invalid ID returns error
- `deleteTodo`: Deletes existing todo, handles non-existent ID
- `toggleTodo`: Flips completion status, handles non-existent ID

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Tests written for all server actions
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Coverage > 90%
- [ ] Tests documented

---

## Story 6.3: Write Integration Tests for Database Operations
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want integration tests that verify database operations
So that I ensure data persistence works correctly

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the Prisma client and test database
- When I run integration tests
- Then tests verify:
  - Todo can be created and retrieved
  - Todo can be updated
  - Todo can be deleted
  - Queries return correctly sorted results
  - Transactions maintain data integrity
- And tests use a separate test database
- And tests clean up after themselves

### Technical Notes
- Set up test database (SQLite in memory or separate file)
- Use beforeEach/afterEach for cleanup
- Test actual database operations

### Test Cases to Implement
- Create → Read: Todo persists correctly
- Update: All fields update correctly
- Delete: Todo removed from database
- Sorting: Correct order returned
- Concurrent operations: No race conditions

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Test database configured
- [ ] Integration tests written
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Cleanup logic working

---

## Story 6.4: Write E2E Test for Create-Complete-Delete Flow
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want an E2E test for the critical user flow
So that I can verify the application works end-to-end

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the application is running
- When the E2E test executes
- Then it:
  1. Loads the homepage
  2. Fills out the todo form
  3. Submits the form
  4. Verifies the todo appears in the active section
  5. Clicks the checkbox to complete it
  6. Verifies it moves to the completed section
  7. Clicks delete and confirms
  8. Verifies it disappears from the list
- And each step has assertions
- And the test runs in headless mode

### Technical Notes
- Use Playwright or Cypress
- Run against dev server or production build
- Use test selectors (data-testid) if needed

### Definition of Done
- [ ] E2E test script written
- [ ] Test covers full user journey
- [ ] Test runs reliably
- [ ] Screenshots captured on failure

---

## Story 6.5: Write E2E Test for Form Validation
**Priority:** SHOULD HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want to verify form validation works correctly
So that invalid data cannot be submitted

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the application is running
- When the E2E test executes
- Then it:
  1. Attempts to submit empty form
  2. Verifies error message appears
  3. Verifies todo is not created
  4. Fills in title field
  5. Verifies error message disappears
  6. Submits successfully
  7. Verifies todo appears
- And all assertions pass

### Definition of Done
- [ ] E2E validation test written
- [ ] Test covers validation flows
- [ ] Test passes consistently

---

## Story 6.6: Implement Accessibility Automated Testing
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want automated accessibility testing
So that I maintain WCAG AA compliance

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the application pages
- When accessibility tests run
- Then they check for:
  - Color contrast violations
  - Missing alt text
  - Improper heading hierarchy
  - Missing form labels
  - Keyboard navigation issues
- And tests use axe-core or similar tool
- And tests fail if violations found

### Technical Notes
- Use @axe-core/playwright or Lighthouse CI
- Run on all major pages/states
- Integrate into CI/CD

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Accessibility tests configured
- [ ] Tests run on all pages
- [ ] No violations in test results
- [ ] CI/CD integration complete

---

## Story 6.7: Implement Performance Testing
**Priority:** SHOULD HAVE
**Story Points:** 3
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want to verify performance benchmarks
So that the app remains fast

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given the application
- When performance tests run
- Then they verify:
  - Page load time < 2 seconds
  - Time to Interactive < 3 seconds
  - First Contentful Paint < 1 second
  - Form submission response < 500ms
- And tests run against production build
- And tests fail if thresholds exceeded

### Technical Notes
- Use Lighthouse CI or similar
- Run on production build
- Test with throttled connection (simulated 4G)

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Performance tests configured
- [ ] Thresholds defined
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] CI/CD integration complete

---

## Story 6.8: Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with Automated Tests
**Priority:** MUST HAVE
**Story Points:** 5
**Status:** NOT STARTED

As a developer
I want tests to run automatically on every commit
So that I catch issues before deployment

### Acceptance Criteria
- Given a CI/CD platform (GitHub Actions, Azure Pipelines, etc.)
- When code is pushed or PR is created
- Then the pipeline:
  1. Installs dependencies
  2. Runs linting
  3. Runs unit tests
  4. Runs integration tests
  5. Runs E2E tests
  6. Runs accessibility tests
  7. Generates coverage report
  8. Blocks merge if tests fail
- And pipeline completes in < 10 minutes

### Technical Notes
- Use GitHub Actions or Azure Pipelines
- Run tests in parallel where possible
- Cache dependencies for speed

### Definition of Done
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline configured
- [ ] All test suites run
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] Branch protection rules set

---

## User Story Summary

### Completed Stories (29)
- Epic 1: Core Data Infrastructure (7/7)
- Epic 2: Task Creation & Management (5/5)
- Epic 3: Task Display & Organization (4/4)
- Epic 4: Task Completion Workflow (3/3)
- Epic 5: UI/UX Polish & Accessibility (5/5)

### Incomplete Stories (8)
- Epic 6: Quality Assurance & Testing (0/8)

### Total Story Points
- Completed: ~75 points
- Remaining: ~37 points
- Total: ~112 points

### Quality Gaps per Story
Most completed stories are missing:
- [ ] Unit tests
- [ ] Integration tests
- [ ] Component tests
- [ ] E2E tests
- [ ] Accessibility verification
- [ ] Performance verification

---

## Next Actions for Azure DevOps

### Recommended Story Creation Order
1. Story 6.1: Set Up Testing Infrastructure (blocker for others)
2. Story 6.2: Unit Tests for Server Actions (highest risk area)
3. Story 6.4: E2E Test for Critical Flow (validates core functionality)
4. Story 6.6: Accessibility Testing (compliance requirement)
5. Story 6.3: Integration Tests (database reliability)
6. Story 6.5: E2E Validation Test (user experience)
7. Story 6.7: Performance Testing (optimization)
8. Story 6.8: CI/CD Pipeline (automation)

### Azure DevOps Work Item Fields
When creating in ADO, populate:
- **Title:** Story title from above
- **Description:** Full story with acceptance criteria
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Tab-separated Given-When-Then
- **Story Points:** As specified
- **Priority:** 1 (Must), 2 (Should), 3 (Could)
- **Area Path:** Todo-App
- **Iteration:** Next sprint
- **Assigned To:** [Developer name]
- **Tags:** testing, quality-assurance, technical-debt

---

**Document Status:** This user story document was created retroactively to document an already-implemented application. Stories marked "Completed" represent work that was done without formal story definition. Stories marked "Not Started" represent the quality assurance work that still needs to be completed.
